item.title: Solar Surpasses Gas in California After Panel Installs Surge
item.description: Solar power is surpassing natural gas generation in California, demonstrating the sustained and growing impact of renewable energy in the biggest US market for photovoltaic panels.

item.title: US Home Solar Takes Hit After Losing Key Tax Credit
item.description: California and Florida are bucking the trend.

item.title: Texas Flood Threat Persists as Gulf System Nears Storm Status
item.description: The threat of floods continues across Texas, northeast Mexico and the US Gulf Coast as tropical moisture pours into the region and the odds rise that the Atlantic’s first named storm of the year could develop late Tuesday or early Wednesday.

item.title: European Heat Wave Pushes French Power Prices to 2023 High
item.description: A record-breaking heat wave is set to build in western Europe this week, leading to so-called tropical nights and driving French power prices higher as cooling demand surges.

item.title: Scientists Found the Coral Reefs That Can Survive Climate Change
item.description: Some coral reef ecosystems have a far greater chance of surviving the effects of climate change than previously understood, according to new global research.

item.title: US Residential Solar Installations Set to Stall for Years
item.description: While most of the country is experiencing a decline, two states are bucking the trend: California and Florida.

item.title: EU Nations Approve Carbon Benchmarks for Handout of Free Permits
item.description: European Union member states approved a controversial update of benchmark values for the bloc’s carbon market, taking a key step toward distribution of free allowances to companies in the flagship emissions trading system.

item.title: US Finalizes $1.6 Billion Loan to Michigan Gas Utility DTE
item.description: The Trump administration has finalized a $1.6 billion loan to Michigan-based utility DTE Energy Co. to modernize hundreds of miles of natural gas infrastructure.

item.title: Extreme Weather Makes US Tomatoes More Expensive This Summer
item.description: A freeze in Florida and weather issues in Mexico drove prices higher earlier this year.

item.title: ‘Gliderboy’ Reinvents Humble Weather Balloon With Flight Home
item.description: Yohan Hadji, nicknamed `Gliderboy,’ has invented a lightweight foam glider that can bring sensors from weather balloons back from the stratosphere.

item.title: Floods Threaten Texas and Louisiana This Week
item.description: Louisiana and Texas, including Houston, will get hit with flooding rains for the next few days as a potential tropical storm tries to spin up across the western Gulf.

item.title: KKR-Backed Firm Raises $1.3 Billion in Debt for EVs, Batteries
item.description: Zenobe Energy Ltd., a company backed by KKR & Co., has raised £980 million ($1.3 billion) of new debt financing to go toward expanding the fleet of green vehicles in the UK and Ireland.

item.title: Wall Street Is Gaining Access to New Catastrophe Models to Help Predict Wars
item.description: As Wall Street races to incorporate war into its risk scenarios, the same people modeling natural catastrophes are now adapting their methodology to help investors, banks and insurers predict military conflicts.

item.title: UK, Japan Plan to Sign £18 Billion Clean Energy Investment Deal
item.description: The UK and Japan are expected to agree on an £18 billion ($24.1 billion) deal that they predict will create new jobs in the offshore wind, infrastructure and financial services sectors, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s office said in a statement Saturday.

item.title: How One Startup Is Trying to Coax Rain From Clouds
item.description: The technology is needed on a drier planet

item.title: China Maps Out Heavy‑Truck Electrification Push With 40% Goal
item.description: China has set out a plan to scale up new‑energy heavy-duty trucks, targeting 40% market penetration and a fleet exceeding 1.6 million vehicles by 2030.

item.title: Wind Groups Fight Pentagon Project Freeze With Billions at Risk
item.description: Renewable energy groups asked a federal judge to order the US Defense Department to lift its freeze on approvals of wind energy projects that has threatened billions of dollars of investments.

item.title: Brazil’s Development Bank Will Make New Loans to Companies Restoring Native Forests
item.description: The loans totaling $164 million are intended to spur private investment in conservation.

item.title: Extreme Heat Is Already Stifling Economic Growth in India
item.description: Severe temperatures are having increasing impacts on workers and businesses

item.title: Heat Bakes US as World Cup Heads Into Weekend
item.description: Heat is engulfing both the East and West coasts of the US as the World Cup heads into its first weekend, after kicking off in Mexico City on Thursday.

item.title: Total to Seek German Wind Project Compensation Over Grid Delays
item.description: TotalEnergies SE will seek compensation because of uncertainty over the grid-connection timeline for one of its German offshore wind projects, as the company reviews plans for wind farms there.

item.title: Ben & Jerry’s ‘Cannot Criticize’ Trump, Co-Founder Says
item.description: Ben Cohen wants to free the ice cream maker from its owner, Magnum, which he says is holding it back from supporting social causes.

item.title: DHL Says Clean Energy Technology Is Getting Harder to Transport
item.description: Global efforts to transition away from fossil fuels are running into a new challenge: clean energy equipment is getting more complex, hazardous and too big to transport easily.

item.title: US Declares Power Emergency in Southeast as Heat Strains Grids
item.description: The Trump administration declared a power emergency in the southeastern US as forecasters warned of dangerous heat that’s likely to stress power grids along the country’s east coast.

item.title: Gas Supply Woes Are Delaying Bangladesh’s Industrial Dreams
item.description: Inside a new Bangladeshi glass factory with a production floor nearly twice the size of a soccer field, machinery sits wrapped in tarpaulins. At a neighboring steel mill, construction has slowed and production has yet to begin.

item.title: Why Your Summer Tomatoes Cost So Much
item.description: Prices are soaring after crop losses in Mexico and Florida, offering a glimpse of how climate disruptions could make food inflation longer lasting.

item.title: Scientists Urge Countries to Look Beyond CO2 to Tackle Warming
item.description: While carbon dioxide and methane are responsible for the majority of climate change, “indirect” greenhouse gases play a larger role than previously understood.

item.title: El Niño Slams Into a Global Economy Unprepared for More Chaos
item.description: Scientists warn a blockbuster weather event risks pushing costs higher for everything from building materials to groceries.

item.title: EDF Warns of French Nuclear Outages as Rhone River Heats Up
item.description: Electricite de France SA said it may be forced to curb nuclear output at the Saint-Alban nuclear site next week, a first sign that hotter weather risks tightening French power supplies this summer.

item.title: A Rare ‘Super’ El Niño Is Looking More Likely. Here’s What to Expect
item.description: ​​A cyclical weather phenomenon that can drive flooding in one continent and drought in another has returned.
